openmeter/notification.purpose: Domain contract layer for the notification subsystem: defines all types (Channel, Rule, Event, EventPayload, delivery status), service interfaces (Service = ChannelService + RuleService + EventService + FeatureService), and EventHandler lifecycle interface. Sub-packages own persistence, HTTP, async dispatch, and Kafka consumer — this root package is the shared vocabulary they all import.

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertions on every domain type
patterns.description: Every Input type declares `var _ models.Validator = (*XInput)(nil)` and `var _ models.CustomValidator[XInput] = (*XInput)(nil)` to guarantee Validate() and ValidateWith() are implemented.

patterns.name: models.NewNillableGenericValidationError wrapping errors.Join
patterns.description: All Validate() methods accumulate errors into a []error slice and return models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)) — never return raw errors or construct error types directly.

patterns.name: Union config/payload types with type-discriminated Validate()
patterns.description: ChannelConfig, RuleConfig, and EventPayload are union structs with a *Meta type field; Validate() switches on the discriminator and delegates to the sub-type validator. Adding a new event/channel type requires updating both the union struct and the Validate() switch.
patterns.example: func (c RuleConfig) Validate() error { switch c.Type { case EventTypeBalanceThreshold: return c.BalanceThreshold.Validate() ... } }

patterns.name: EventPayloadVersionCurrent = 1 on all new event payloads
patterns.description: EventPayloadMeta.Version must be set to EventPayloadVersionCurrent (1) when constructing payloads for new events. The adapter reads version to apply migration logic for older payloads.

patterns.name: MaxChannelsPerRule constant enforced in Input.Validate()
patterns.description: CreateRuleInput and UpdateRuleInput check len(Channels) <= MaxChannelsPerRule (5) inside Validate() — never inline the constant.
patterns.example: if len(i.Channels) > MaxChannelsPerRule { errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(...)) }

patterns.name: eventTypes slice as the single registry for valid EventType values
patterns.description: EventType.Validate() uses lo.Contains(eventTypes, t). Adding a new EventType requires appending to the eventTypes package-level var in event.go — not just adding a const.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Service interface as composition of ChannelService, RuleService, EventService, and FeatureService. All HTTP handlers and consumers depend on notification.Service — never depend on the concrete service struct.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding methods directly to Service instead of a sub-interface breaks the smallest-surface dependency principle.

key_file_guides.role: EventPayload union type + EventPayloadVersionCurrent constant. eventPayload.Validate() is the entry-point for payload correctness checks.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New event type added to EventPayload struct without updating Validate() switch — payload passes validation silently.

key_file_guides.role: EventHandler interface (EventDispatcher + EventReconciler + Start/Close). Defines DefaultReconcileInterval, DefaultDispatchTimeout, DefaultDeliveryStatePendingTimeout, DefaultDeliveryStateSendingTimeout.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Implementations must not block in Dispatch — it is a fire-and-forget contract.

key_file_guides.role: eventTypes registry, EventType, Event struct, all *EventInput types. Contains the canonical list of valid event types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New EventType const without appending to eventTypes slice — EventType.Validate() will reject it.

key_file_guides.role: Channel and Rule domain types plus all CRUD input types. ChannelConfig and RuleConfig union types with discriminated Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new channel/rule type without handling it in the corresponding Config.Validate() switch.

key_file_guides.role: Repository interface (ChannelRepository + RuleRepository + EventRepository + entutils.TxCreator) — the persistence contract. Implemented by the adapter sub-package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never import the adapter sub-package from this package — Repository is the interface, adapter is the implementation.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic (Svix calls, DB queries) to this package — it is a types+interfaces package; logic belongs in service/ or adapter/ sub-packages.
Defining a new EventType const without adding it to the eventTypes slice in event.go — breaks EventType.Validate().
Returning raw errors from Validate() instead of models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(...)) — breaks HTTP 400 mapping.
Adding a new payload variant to EventPayload struct without updating EventPayload.Validate() switch — new payloads silently bypass validation.
Importing openmeter/notification/adapter, /service, or /webhook/svix from this package — creates import cycles; callers depend on interfaces, not implementations.

decisions.decision: Root package contains only types, interfaces, and constants — no implementations.
decisions.rationale: All sub-packages (adapter, service, httpdriver, consumer, eventhandler, webhook) import this package for shared types; putting any implementation here would create import cycles.

decisions.decision: EventPayloadVersionCurrent is a single int constant rather than per-event versioning.
decisions.rationale: Versioning is global to simplify migration logic in the adapter; the adapter only needs to branch on version for event types whose schema changed (currently invoice).

decisions.decision: EventHandler is composed of EventDispatcher and EventReconciler sub-interfaces.
decisions.rationale: Allows the balance-worker and other callers that only need to dispatch (not reconcile) to depend on the smaller EventDispatcher interface.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new event type: extend eventTypes registry and union types

openmeter/sink.purpose: High-throughput Kafka→ClickHouse sink worker: consumes raw CloudEvent messages from Kafka partitions, deduplicates via Redis or in-memory, batch-inserts into ClickHouse via streaming.Connector, then fires post-flush callbacks (FlushEventHandler) so the balance-worker can recalculate entitlements. Primary constraint: exactly-once guarantee requires strict flush ordering (ClickHouse → Kafka offset commit → Redis dedupe).

patterns.name: SinkConfig.Validate() before any Sink construction
patterns.description: All Sink configuration is validated in SinkConfig.Validate() before NewSink returns. Every required field has an explicit nil/zero check; callers must not pass partially-configured SinkConfig.
patterns.example: func NewSink(config SinkConfig) (*Sink, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf(...) } }

patterns.name: Three-phase flush ordering: storage → offset commit → Redis dedupe
patterns.description: flush() always writes ClickHouse first, then stores Kafka offsets, then sets Redis dedupe keys. Reversing this order breaks the exactly-once guarantee.

patterns.name: FlushEventHandler called in a goroutine after flush, never blocking
patterns.description: OnFlushSuccess is dispatched in a separate goroutine with a FlushSuccessTimeout context. Never call FlushEventHandler synchronously from flush().
patterns.example: go func() { ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, s.config.FlushSuccessTimeout); defer cancel(); s.config.FlushEventHandler.OnFlushSuccess(ctx, messages) }()

patterns.name: NamespacedMeterCache for meter definitions, refreshed periodically
patterns.description: Meter definitions are cached in NamespacedMeterCache and periodically re-fetched at MeterRefetchInterval. Access meter definitions via meterCache.GetMetersByType(), never query meter.Service directly in the hot path.

patterns.name: Storage interface for ClickHouse — inject via SinkConfig.Storage
patterns.description: ClickHouseStorage implements Storage. Tests can inject a mock. Never instantiate ClickHouseStorage directly inside sink.go.
patterns.example: type Storage interface { BatchInsert(ctx context.Context, messages []sinkmodels.SinkMessage) error }

key_file_guides.role: Main Sink struct, SinkConfig, NewSink constructor, flush() orchestration, Run() event loop. The flush mutex (s.mu) serialises partition pause/resume with close.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any change to flush ordering risks exactly-once violation; the mu.Lock covers the full ClickHouse+offset+Redis sequence.

key_file_guides.role: Storage interface and ClickHouseStorage implementation. Reads ingested_at Kafka header; generates a ULID StoreRowID per event.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ingested_at header parse failure returns an error stopping the entire batch — validate header format at ingest time.

key_file_guides.role: SinkBuffer: mutex-guarded map keyed by TopicPartition for deduplication within a batch. Add is idempotent on same TopicPartition+Offset.
key_file_guides.watch_for: RemoveByPartitions used on partition revocation — callers must call this before rebalance completes.

key_file_guides.role: NamespacedMeterCache: RWMutex-guarded namespace→MetersByType map with atomic isRunning flag. Periodic refresh goroutine started by Start().
key_file_guides.watch_for: isRunning must be set before starting the ticker goroutine; double-start is a no-op but the cache will not refresh.

key_file_guides.role: SinkMessage (pipeline carrier), ProcessingStatus/ProcessingState (OK/DROP). Pure types package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add business logic here; importing streaming or meter from models creates a circular dependency.

key_file_guides.role: FlushEventHandler interface + buffered async impl. Two-phase shutdown: stopChan signal then drain loop.
key_file_guides.watch_for: WaitForDrain() must be called before process exit or buffered messages will be dropped.

anti_patterns: Changing flush() to write Redis dedupe before Kafka offset commit — violates exactly-once guarantee on consumer restart.
Calling FlushEventHandler.OnFlushSuccess synchronously inside flush() — blocks the main sink loop and causes backpressure on Kafka partitions.
Querying meter.Service directly inside the hot Kafka consume path instead of NamespacedMeterCache — causes per-message DB round trips.
Constructing ClickHouseStorage inside sink.go instead of injecting via SinkConfig.Storage — breaks testability.
Adding new ProcessingState iota values without implementing ProcessingState.String() and handling them in persistToStorage() — results in an 'unknown state type' error at runtime.

decisions.decision: Flush is protected by a mutex that also covers partition pause/resume.
decisions.rationale: Prevents the sink from closing mid-flush when a rebalance arrives; ensures ClickHouse write + offset commit + Redis dedupe are atomic from the consumer's perspective.

decisions.decision: Storage is an interface injected via SinkConfig, not instantiated inside Sink.
decisions.rationale: Allows test doubles for ClickHouse without spinning up a real ClickHouse instance; aligns with the layered adapter pattern used across the codebase.

decisions.decision: FlushEventHandler dispatched in a goroutine with a FlushSuccessTimeout.
decisions.rationale: Post-flush notification (for balance recalculation) must not block the main ingest path; a timeout bounds the goroutine lifetime even if downstream is slow.

code_examples.scenario: Implementing a new FlushEventHandler for post-flush side effects

openmeter/watermill.purpose: Kafka-backed pub-sub abstraction layer for all async domain event passing between OpenMeter binaries. Owns three named topics (ingest, system, balance-worker), topic routing by event-name prefix, CloudEvents wire format, standard Watermill router middleware stack, and driver selection (kafka/noop). All domain event producers and consumers depend on sub-packages here — never on raw Watermill or confluent-kafka-go primitives.

patterns.name: Always publish via eventbus.Publisher — never directly to a Kafka topic string
patterns.description: Topic routing (ingest vs system vs balance-worker) is determined by event-name prefix inside eventbus. Publishing directly to a Kafka topic bypasses this routing and breaks consumer group isolation.

patterns.name: All workers use NewDefaultRouter as their router factory
patterns.description: NewDefaultRouter wires the fixed middleware stack (PoisonQueue, DLQ telemetry, CorrelationID, Recoverer, Retry, ProcessingTimeout+RestoreContext, HandlerMetrics). Never construct *message.Router directly.

patterns.name: Event types registered via grouphandler.NewGroupEventHandler — unknown types silently dropped
patterns.description: NoPublishingHandler dispatches to registered GroupEventHandlers keyed by CloudEvents ce_type header. Unregistered event types are dropped, not errored, so workers are tolerant of schema evolution.

patterns.name: CloudEvents 1.0 wire format via marshaler.CloudEventMarshaler
patterns.description: All events must implement the Event interface (EventName, EventMetadata, Validate). Marshal sets ce_type from EventName(); never set CloudEventsHeaderType manually in message metadata.

patterns.name: EventVersionSubsystem prefix determines topic routing
patterns.description: eventbus.GeneratePublishTopic matches event name prefixes against subsystem constants. New events must have EventName() starting with a recognised subsystem prefix; otherwise they silently route to SystemEventsTopic.

key_file_guides.role: Publisher interface and concrete EventBus with topic routing by event-name prefix. Single injection point for all event producers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Event names without a registered subsystem prefix silently route to SystemEventsTopic — verify prefix mapping when adding new event families.

key_file_guides.role: NoPublishingHandler multiplexer. Unknown event types are dropped. AddHandler is safe to call after construction (RWMutex-guarded).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never return errors for unknown event types — causes infinite Watermill retries and DLQ poisoning.

key_file_guides.role: CloudEvents 1.0 serializer/deserializer. Calls Validate() on deserialized events. ULID auto-ID if ID is empty; time.Now() if Time is zero.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Implementations of Event.Validate() that are too strict can reject valid events from older producers during rolling deploys.

key_file_guides.role: NewDefaultRouter: fixed middleware order. MaxRetries off-by-one: config value 0 = 1 attempt, 1 = 2 attempts.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add Timeout middleware without RestoreContext immediately before it (watermill#467 workaround).

key_file_guides.role: Organizational parent for kafka/ (IBM Sarama-backed) and noop/ driver implementations. Wire selects driver via config flag.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No source files belong directly in driver/ — only in kafka/ or noop/ sub-packages.

key_file_guides.role: Adapter utilities between Watermill's NoPublishHandlerFunc and HandlerFunc. Use HandlerFuncToNoPublisherHandler only when the inner handler never produces messages.
key_file_guides.watch_for: HandlerFuncToNoPublisherHandler panics with ErrMessagesProduced if the adapted handler returns messages — guard this assumption.

anti_patterns: Constructing a *message.Router directly instead of router.NewDefaultRouter — middleware stack (DLQ, OTel, Retry) will be missing.
Defining event names without a stable EventVersionSubsystem prefix — routing silently falls through to SystemEventsTopic.
Adding source files directly in openmeter/watermill/driver/ — all driver code must live in kafka/ or noop/ sub-packages.
Setting MaxRetries=0 in router.Options intending 'no retries' — the subtraction logic results in 1 attempt; use 1 for single-attempt.
Publishing directly to a raw Kafka topic string from domain code — bypasses eventbus routing and breaks consumer group isolation.

decisions.decision: Three fixed Kafka topics with prefix-based routing inside eventbus rather than per-event topic configuration.
decisions.rationale: Topic isolation matches the worker topology (ingest/balance/system); centralising routing in eventbus prevents producers from accidentally writing to the wrong consumer group.

decisions.decision: CloudEvents 1.0 as the wire format for all Watermill messages.
decisions.rationale: Provides a standard schema for ce_type header-based routing in grouphandler and interoperability with Svix webhook payloads.

decisions.decision: Unknown event types are silently dropped in NoPublishingHandler rather than errored.
decisions.rationale: Allows independent deployment of producers and consumers — a consumer can safely ignore event types it has not yet registered without poisoning its DLQ.

code_examples.scenario: Registering a new event handler in a worker's NoPublishingHandler
